profiles.description: Jimi Hendrix's purpose-built Greenwich Village studio. Distinctive curved walls, Neve 8068 consoles, warm low-mid resonance. Landmark for jazz-fusion, R&B, and classic rock.

console.model: Neve 8078 (Studio A, restored with 1081 modules) / SSL 9080 custom (Studio B) / Neve 8068 (Studio C)

eq_preference.character: silky high shelves, musical low-end warmth

mix_tendencies.low_end: Full and warm without muddiness. 60–80 Hz foundation with careful 200–300 Hz management.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Smooth, never harsh. Upper mids (2–4 kHz) kept polite to serve vocals.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Airy 'Neve silk' — 10 kHz and above lifted gently without sibilance.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Musical. Compression used for glue rather than limiting. Transients preserved.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Wide but coherent. Room ambience adds natural width.
signature_moves: Pultec low-shelf boost at 60 Hz with simultaneous cut to tighten
Neve 1073 as the tonal foundation, then EQ out of mix
Parallel compression to preserve transient feel
Lexicon 480L for lush stereo room on mix bus

api5500.rationale: Gentle 60 Hz foundation boost, light 200 Hz warmth, subtle 10 kHz air — the Neve 8068 character translated to API-style fixed bands

buttercomp2.rationale: Gentle glue compression (~2:1), medium attack to preserve transients, program-dependent release, 75% wet for parallel feel

pultec.rationale: Classic Pultec push-pull at 60 Hz for warm foundation with tightness. 10 kHz high shelf boost for Neve-style silk

dynamic_eq.rationale: Light dynamic control — this profile favors natural dynamics. DynEQ used only to catch occasional build-ups, not as a primary shaping tool

transformer.rationale: Neve transformer character (model 0.66 = Neve position). Light drive preserves the warmth without aggressive saturation

punch.rationale: Soft ceiling, conservative transient shaping. The Electric Lady sound lives in dynamics — don't over-limit

profiles.description: Former Consolidated Edison substation. Massive live room with 55-foot ceiling, legendary for huge drum sounds. SSL 4000 and 9000 consoles. The definitive 80s rock and R&B sound.

console.model: Neve 8068 Mk.II (Studio A, 40-input) / SSL 4000E (Studios B & C) / SSL 9000J (current flagship)

eq_preference.character: surgical and punchy, not musical — carve the mix rather than sweeten it

mix_tendencies.low_end: Tight and punchy. The room's physical size provides natural low-end extension — mix benefits from controlled 80–120 Hz.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Forward and present. Upper mids pushed to cut through dense arrangements.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Bright and present. SSL sheen without harshness.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Compressed. SSL G-Bus doing real work (4:1, 3ms attack, auto release). GR visible.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Wide. The live room adds natural depth; mixes rarely need artificial width.
signature_moves: SSL G-Bus at 4:1 with fast attack, auto release, 2–4 dB GR
Roll off below 30 Hz aggressively on master bus
High-pass filter on everything except kick and bass
Bright, forward vocal presence boosted at 3–5 kHz

api5500.rationale: SSL character: cut clutter below 40 Hz, gentle warmth at 150 Hz, presence push at 3–5 kHz, SSL air boost at 8–10 kHz

buttercomp2.rationale: SSL G-Bus emulation: 4:1 ratio, fast attack (3ms), auto release, full wet — this is the signature Power Station bus glue

pultec.rationale: Conservative Pultec here — SSL is the primary tonal shaper. Light low-end tightening, moderate high presence

dynamic_eq.rationale: More active DynEQ — controlling the live room low-end buildup and keeping the midrange forward during dense passages

transformer.rationale: SSL transformer model (position 1.0). Light drive — SSL already provides its own saturation character in the bus compression

punch.rationale: Slightly harder ceiling. Higher transient attack enhancement to match Power Station's punchy drum-forward aesthetic

profiles.description: Hollywood institution founded by Tutti Camarata. Small-room sound known for intimate warmth and a beloved reverb chamber. Neve and SSL rooms. Landmark for classic rock, pop, and metal.

console.model: Custom API/DeMedio with API 990 op-amps + API 550A EQ (Studios 1 & 3) / Neve 8088 with 1081 modules (Studio 2)

mix_tendencies.low_end: Warm and round. Sunset's small-room character adds congestion at 200 Hz — mixes often subtract here.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Intimate. Guitars and vocals sit close; mixes rarely need to push presence.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Neutral to dark. The room doesn't gift you high-end extension — it's earned.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Variable. Classic rock recordings here are uncompressed and loud; pop has more bus processing.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Tight width on rock. The room's reverb chamber adds mono-compatible depth.
signature_moves: The Sunset reverb chamber — a physical chamber that appears on countless recordings
Mid-side processing to open up the center
1176 in 'British mode' (all-buttons) on aggressive buses
HPF below 30 Hz + gentle 200–250 Hz notch to clear the small room buildup

api5500.rationale: Warm low foundation, gentle 200 Hz sculpt (room buildup control), slight upper-mid presence, conservative air — Sunset's intimate character

buttercomp2.rationale: FET character (1176 reference): moderate ratio ~3:1, fast attack, medium release. 85% wet keeps the transient snap

pultec.rationale: Pultec push-pull for warm low foundation. Conservative high-end boost — Sunset's rooms aren't bright

dynamic_eq.rationale: Dynamic control at 200 Hz (small-room buildup) and mid frequencies — more active than Electric Lady, less than Power Station

transformer.rationale: API transformer character (0.33). Moderate drive — Sunset mixing often uses API outboard heavily

punch.rationale: Balanced transient shaping. Sunset records span classic rock (preserve dynamics) to modern pop (tighter ceiling)

profiles.description: John McBride's world-class Nashville facility. Multiple large-format rooms with modern SSL and Neve consoles alongside vintage gear. Known for acoustic excellence and live-to-tape recording.

console.model: Neve 8078 (Studio A, ex-Motown LA, fully rebuilt — 1081 EQ modules) / SSL Origin (recent addition)

eq_preference.character: transparent when needed, colored when desired — Nashville serves the song

mix_tendencies.low_end: Defined and musical. Nashville mixes prioritize clarity — bass and kick share space without congestion.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Open and present. Acoustic instruments need room in the 1–3 kHz range.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Extended and airy. Steel guitar and acoustic overtones benefit from gentle high-end lift.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Natural. Live-performance energy preserved. Gentle glue rather than crushing.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Organic. Room mics and natural width over artificial stereo widening.
signature_moves: Optical compression (Tube-Tech) for transparent, breathing glue
Gentle Pultec at 100 Hz for acoustic warmth
High-pass filtered low mids to let acoustic guitar fundamentals breathe
Subtle tape saturation for analog warmth on digital sessions

api5500.rationale: Serve-the-song EQ: gentle foundation, open midrange, air for steel guitar and acoustic overtones

buttercomp2.rationale: Optical character: slow attack, program-dependent release, gentle ratio (~1.5:1). 70% wet preserves live feel

pultec.rationale: Acoustic warmth at 100 Hz, extended air at 12 kHz. Blackbird's rooms need gentle encouragement, not heavy shaping

dynamic_eq.rationale: Very conservative DynEQ. Nashville top-down mixing values natural dynamics — intervention only when acoustics build up

transformer.rationale: Neve character, very light drive. Blackbird often runs clean signal paths — transformer color here is seasoning, not sauce

punch.rationale: High ceiling (more headroom), gentle transient shaping — preserve the natural dynamics of live performances

profiles.description: Miami's legendary studio. Home to Eric Clapton, Eagles, and the Bee Gees. Known for a deep, sun-drenched low end and Miami bass character. MCI consoles, humid warmth.

mix_tendencies.low_end: Deep and warm. 15 ips tape at +3 saturation gives natural compression and harmonic richness below 100 Hz.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Open and soulful. Miami sessions often feature live horn sections — mids kept uncrowded.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Slightly dark. 15 ips tape rolls off above 12 kHz naturally — accepted as character.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Moderate. Tape compression doing work; bus compressor adds polish rather than control.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Wide with depth. Plate reverb on master bus adds space without washing.
signature_moves: 15 ips tape speed for extra saturation and natural low-end warmth
EMT 140 plate reverb as master bus ambience
API 550A on the mix bus for punchy mid coloration
Deep low-end foundation (60–80 Hz) boosted before tape saturation

api5500.rationale: Miami warmth: deep 60 Hz foundation, 150 Hz warmth, gentle 2 kHz mid presence, moderate 8 kHz — tape rolls off the rest naturally

buttercomp2.rationale: FET/Neve blend: moderate ratio (~2.5:1), medium attack and release. Heavier wet mix because tape is doing compression work too

pultec.rationale: Heavy Pultec low-end push (Criteria low-end character). Conservative high shelf — let tape darkness be the character

dynamic_eq.rationale: Moderate DynEQ to manage the live room's low-end warmth and keep the deep bass character without overwhelming smaller speakers

transformer.rationale: API character (0.33) with moderate drive — Criteria's MCI/API combination gives a warm, slightly punchy character

punch.rationale: Moderate ceiling and transient shaping — the tape compression and heavy low-end need some management to translate on modern playback

landmark_records.album: Layla and Other Assorted Love Songs

profiles.description: Guitarist/producer who owns Mana Recording in St. Petersburg, FL. Known for massive, surgical death metal mixes: tight low-end, brutal midrange presence, and controlled aggression. Clients include Cannibal Corpse, Morbid Angel, Obituary.

mix_tendencies.low_end: Tight and controlled. Sub frequencies (below 60 Hz) aggressively filtered. The weight lives at 80–120 Hz.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Brutal and forward. Guitars cut at 300–400 Hz (mud) and pushed at 700–900 Hz (grind). Vocals sit right of center.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Defined and present. Hi-hat and cymbal detail without wash. High-frequency air is minimal — this music doesn't need it.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Heavily compressed. Consistent, controlled aggression. Little to no dynamic variation on the bus.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Wide guitars, centered drums. Classic death metal panning.
signature_moves: Aggressive HPF on the master bus below 30 Hz
API 2500 at 4:1+ with fast attack/release for relentless control
Brutal 300–400 Hz cut on guitar buses before they hit the master
Kick drum centered and wide — single dominant element at 80 Hz
Parallel distortion on bass for mid presence

api5500.rationale: Rutan's surgical approach: cut below 40 Hz, cut mud at 200 Hz, push grind at 700-900 Hz, push presence at 2–3 kHz, moderate definition at 8 kHz

buttercomp2.rationale: Heavy VCA compression — high ratio (6:1+), very fast attack and release. Relentless bus control. Full wet — no parallel blend

pultec.rationale: Pultec as a tightening tool: the push-pull at 100 Hz tightens the low end. High-end boost adds definition on cymbals and pick attack

dynamic_eq.rationale: Aggressive dynamic control across all bands. Rutan's mixes are consistent — DynEQ actively preventing low-end buildup and midrange masking

transformer.rationale: API character with moderate-to-high drive. API transformers add the punchy coloration associated with Mana Recording's sound

punch.rationale: Hard ceiling, high transient attack (drum attack emphasis), short sustain (tight, controlled). This is death metal — it should be aggressive and controlled simultaneously

profiles.description: The architects of 80s-90s R&B. Flyte Tyme Productions, Minneapolis. Signature sound: deep programmed bass, lush string synths, punchy drum machines, and impeccable vocal space. Clients: Janet Jackson, Mariah Carey, Usher.

eq_preference.character: precise and clean — the color comes from synthesis, not EQ

mix_tendencies.low_end: Deep and controlled programmed bass. Sub-bass punchy and sustained. Bass and kick locked in phase.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Open for vocals above everything else. Instruments carefully carved to not intrude on vocal range.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Bright and shimmering. Hi-hats and percussion detailed and forward. Airy without harshness.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Controlled and polished. SSL bus compression with 2–3 dB GR. Nothing too dynamic — this is produced music.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Lush and wide. String pads pushed wide; drums centered; bass mono.
signature_moves: Vocal sits above everything — cut anything that intrudes at 800 Hz–2 kHz
SSL G-Bus at 2:1, 10ms attack, auto release — polished glue
Deep programmed bass sits at 50–80 Hz, barely touched
Lexicon 480L stereo room adds depth without muddying
Drum machine samples EQed to sound like real kit — hi-hat brightness key

api5500.rationale: Deep bass foundation, mid carve to open vocal space, presence lift for percussion detail, extended air for the Flyte Tyme sheen

buttercomp2.rationale: SSL G-Bus character: 2:1 gentle, medium attack, program release. Nearly full wet — the polished production feel requires consistent dynamics

pultec.rationale: Low-end weight for the programmed bass. High-end lift at 12–16 kHz for the characteristic Jam & Lewis shimmer on hi-hats and synths

dynamic_eq.rationale: Moderate DynEQ controlling frequency build-up during chorus sections where multiple synth layers stack up

transformer.rationale: SSL character (1.0), minimal drive. Jam & Lewis mixes are clean and precise — transformer color is very subtle

punch.rationale: Balanced ceiling and transient shaping — drum machine hits need attack definition but programmed music benefits from sustain control

landmark_records.producer: Jimmy Jam & Terry Lewis / Lil Jon

profiles.description: What if Erik Rutan applied his surgical death metal precision to an R&B record? The result: unusually tight low-end control, aggressive mid-frequency carving for vocal clarity, and heavy VCA compression giving a relentlessly polished feel. The warmth is hard-won rather than inherited.

eq_preference.character: surgical clarity applied to soul music — unusual precision, not lush warmth

mix_tendencies.low_end: Rutan's tight 80–120 Hz weight applied to programmed/live bass. Surprisingly controlled for R&B.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Death-metal-style vocal clarity carving (300–500 Hz cut) benefits the vocal sit — paradoxically cleaner than typical R&B.
mix_tendencies.high_end: More restrained than typical R&B. Rutan would add definition, not shimmer. Hi-hats present but not sparkly.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Heavier bus compression than typical R&B — VCA glue applied assertively. More controlled, less breathy.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Death metal panning habits meet R&B: wider lows than expected, more centered vocals.
signature_moves: Aggressive HPF on everything except kick and bass — much higher cutoffs than R&B producers typically use
Surgical 300–400 Hz cut to clear 'mud' — creates unusual vocal presence for the genre
API compression on bus at higher ratio than typical R&B (3–4:1 vs standard 2:1)
Pultec low-end push at 60 Hz — the one concession to genre warmth

api5500.rationale: Rutan's surgical cuts adapted: tight low foundation, mud cut at 200 Hz, presence push at 700 Hz for vocal clarity, upper presence — stops short of metal aggression

buttercomp2.rationale: Rutan's heavy-handed compression applied with R&B restraint: moderate-high ratio (4:1), fast-medium attack, medium release. Still harder than typical R&B

pultec.rationale: The genre concession: warm Pultec low-end that Rutan would use, but with the push-pull tightening he favors. High end stays controlled

dynamic_eq.rationale: Aggressive DynEQ reflecting Rutan's control-oriented approach, but with slightly higher thresholds to allow R&B vocal dynamics to breathe

transformer.rationale: API transformer (Rutan's console of choice) at moderate drive — more color than R&B typically uses, less than death metal

punch.rationale: Harder ceiling than R&B norm (Rutan tendency), strong transient attack for drum clarity — the death-metal instinct of making every hit count

combination.blend_notes: Engineer authority dominates compression, transient shaping, and EQ surgery. Genre shift softens the extremes: ratio from 6:1 to 4:1, ceiling raised from 0.75 to 0.78, Pultec low-end added (genre concession). The result is unusual — an R&B mix that sounds startlingly clear and controlled compared to typical genre production.

profiles.display_name: Cannibal Corpse at Electric Lady Studios
profiles.description: Cannibal Corpse's brutal death metal run through Electric Lady's Neve 8068 and its warm, live room. The room's natural reverb meets the band's tight death metal aesthetic. The conflict: Neve warmth vs. death metal precision. Resolution: the studio's live acoustics are tamed while the Neve transformer color adds unexpected depth to an already brutal sound.

eq_preference.character: conflict resolved: Neve warmth for low-end, API precision for surgical cuts

notable_outboard: Lexicon 480L (NOT used — live room provides ambience)

mix_tendencies.low_end: The studio's warm low-end conflicts with Rutan's tight approach. Resolution: Neve warmth at 80–100 Hz kept, but below 60 Hz filtered aggressively. The result is a death metal mix with unusual depth.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Rutan's precision carving still active (300–400 Hz cut), but the Neve 1073 adds a warm harmonic richness that makes guitars sound different — fuller, less brittle.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Here the studio wins: the Neve 8068's 'silk' and the Lexicon 480L's air add an unusual sheen to death metal — cymbals and guitar harmonics have unexpected beauty.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Engineer dominates: heavy VCA compression still present. But the tape saturation (Studer A80) adds natural compression that makes the overall dynamics less relentless.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: The live room adds natural width that Mana Recording lacks. Guitars in this scenario would be wider and the room would add depth to cymbals.
signature_moves: Neve 8068 transformer warmth applied to guitar buses — unexpected harmonic depth for death metal
Studer A80 tape saturation at +3 adds natural compression on top of VCA bus compression
Electric Lady's live room ambience on drum overheads — NOT gated (unusual for death metal)
Neve 33609 in parallel with aggressive VCA — blending warmth into brutal compression
High-end 'Neve silk' EQ on bus — cymbals have unexpected beauty

api5500.rationale: Hybrid: studio's warm 60 Hz boost meets engineer's mud cut at 200 Hz. Rutan's presence push at 700 Hz still active. Studio wins on high-end air (10 kHz elevated more than Rutan typically allows)

buttercomp2.rationale: Engineer dominates compression: high ratio (5:1+), fast attack and release. Slightly reduced from full Rutan intensity to account for tape compression doubling

pultec.rationale: Studio wins on Pultec: Electric Lady's Neve-warm Pultec low-end applied (more boost than Rutan alone). High shelf elevated vs. Rutan's typical approach — studio's 'silk' quality applied to metal

dynamic_eq.rationale: Aggressive DynEQ (engineer) but managing the live room low-end buildup (studio). Active across all bands — this combination creates complex frequency interactions

transformer.rationale: Studio wins: Neve transformer model (0.66). Higher drive than Electric Lady typically uses — meeting the engineer's instinct for more color. The warmth of Neve plus moderate saturation creates unexpected depth on death metal

punch.rationale: Engineer's hard ceiling and strong transient attack (death metal instinct). Slightly higher than Rutan alone because tape and live room are adding natural limiting. Short sustain — tight, controlled aggression

combination.blend_notes: Studio wins on: room acoustics (live reverb, not gated), console character (Neve over API), transformer model, Pultec low-end warmth, high-frequency air. Engineer wins on: compression ratio and speed, transient shaping, punch ceiling hardness, dynamic EQ aggressiveness. Conflict resolved: the Neve's warmth makes guitar harmonics unexpectedly beautiful; the engineer's aggression makes the bus controlled and tight despite the live room. The unexpected result is death metal with depth and harmonic richness that Mana Recording doesn't normally provide.

profiles.description: The birthplace of Florida death metal. SSL 4000G, Scott Burns engineering. Clinical precision: triggered drums, scooped guitars, surgical separation. Cannibal Corpse, Death, Morbid Angel, Obituary.

eq_preference.character: surgical mid-scoop on guitars, presence boost, no warmth

mix_tendencies.low_end: Ultra-tight below 150 Hz. Sub aggressively filtered. Kick at 80-100 Hz with zero room bleed.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Scooped guitar (800-1k notch) with presence boost (3-5 kHz). Maximum separation.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Cymbals present and defined. No wash, no reverb tails.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Triggered drums, zero natural ambience. dbx 160A fast compression.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Wide guitars L/R, centered drums. No artificial width.
signature_moves: Triggered kick: zero decay, typewriter aesthetic
SSL G scooped guitar EQ
dbx 160A fast drum bus compression
HPF aggressive at 60 Hz
Minimal reverb gated to 80ms max

api5500.rationale: Scott Burns template: aggressive HP below 40 Hz, mud cut 200 Hz, guitar scoop 700 Hz, presence push 2-3 kHz, definition 8 kHz

buttercomp2.rationale: dbx 160A character: very high ratio 8:1+, blazing fast attack/release, full wet. Relentless consistency

pultec.rationale: Minimal Pultec: push-pull tightens low end further. High shelf for cymbal definition

dynamic_eq.rationale: Maximum DynEQ across all bands. Morrisound is surgically controlled

transformer.rationale: SSL character (1.0), minimal drive. SSL G is transparent

punch.rationale: Hard ceiling, maximum transient attack, minimal sustain for tight-gate aesthetic

profiles.description: Chris Blackwell Island Records studio. Neve 8078 and AMS RMX-16 defined the sound: deep groove, tempo-locked reverb delays, warm Neve low-end. AC/DC Back in Black, Talking Heads Remain in Light, Grace Jones Nightclubbing.

eq_preference.character: forward articulate -- Neve warmth plus API punch

mix_tendencies.low_end: Deep syncopated groove. Sly and Robbie bass forward. Neve warmth plus tape adds weight without mud.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Articulate and forward. API 550 bite on guitars and keys.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Bright and present. AMS RMX-16 shimmer. Crisp without harshness.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Groove-forward. Natural dynamics -- feel over compression.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Wide and rhythmic. AMS RMX-16 delays panned wide, tempo-locked.
signature_moves: AMS RMX-16 delay timed to tempo
Neve 8078 three-dimensionality
Sly Dunbar drums set up 4+ hours for maximum care
API 550 bite before Neve summing
Tropical isolation sessions with no time pressure

api5500.rationale: Neve+API hybrid: warm low (Neve), mid body, forward upper-mid articulation (API 550 character), present high end

buttercomp2.rationale: Tube character (Lucas compressor): gentle 1.8:1, medium attack, program release. 70% wet -- feel over control

pultec.rationale: Warm Neve low-end push, moderate high-end air to complement AMS RMX-16 brightness

dynamic_eq.rationale: Light DynEQ. Compass Point is groove-forward, not controlled. Minimal intervention

transformer.rationale: Neve character (0.66) moderate drive. Built on Neve 8078 transformer warmth

punch.rationale: High ceiling, balanced transient shaping. Groove and feel over aggressive limiting

profiles.description: The Swampers studio at 3614 Jackson Highway. UA 610 tube console original era, Neve 8068 later. Dead intimate room. Rolling Stones, Aretha Franklin, Paul Simon. Groove lives in the performances: tube saturation and 15 ips tape do the processing.

eq_preference.character: musical and warm -- tube saturation IS the EQ

mix_tendencies.low_end: Round and warm. 15 ips tape saturation adds natural compression and harmonic richness.
mix_tendencies.midrange: Intimate and vocal-forward. Dead room forces close mixing.
mix_tendencies.high_end: Dark naturally. 15 ips tape rolls off above 12 kHz.
mix_tendencies.dynamics: Natural. Tube saturation and tape do the work. Bus compressor minimal.
mix_tendencies.stereo_field: Narrow. Dead room provides no natural width -- centered, direct, intimate.
signature_moves: UA 610 all-tube path: natural compression at every stage
15 ips tape running hot for heavy saturation
The Swampers locked groove without click
EMT plate for spatial air only
RCA 44-BX ribbon on vocals

api5500.rationale: Tube console character: warm low-end, full body 150-200 Hz, gentle midrange presence. High end restrained -- 15 ips rolls off naturally

buttercomp2.rationale: Very gentle bus compression -- tube saturation and tape already working hard. FET character, slow attack for groove feel. 65% wet

pultec.rationale: Heavy Pultec low-end mimicking UA 610 tube warmth. Conservative high end: warm and dark is the Muscle Shoals character

dynamic_eq.rationale: Minimal DynEQ. Tube saturation and performance dynamics are organic -- not to be over-controlled

transformer.rationale: Trident/neutral (0.0) at high drive -- approximating UA 610 tube preamp saturation character

punch.rationale: High ceiling, gentle transient shaping. 15 ips tape already limiting peaks. High sustain for Swampers groove feel
